Title: Age-Triggered and Dark-Induced Leaf Senescence Require the bHLH Transcription Factors PIF3, 4, and 5

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting; indicator of senescence status
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; phytochrome-interacting factor (PIF) family
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of leaf senescence-associated transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: chlorophyll degradation and senescence-associated metabolic remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive senescence mediator required for full age-triggered and dark-induced chlorophyll loss
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes senescence marker induction and chlorophyll loss during senescence
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: phytochromes (contextual family interaction implied by PIF identity)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (pif3/SALK_081927C); overexpression analysis; chlorophyll measurement; Fv/Fm measurement; ion leakage assay; qRT-PCR; DAB staining; ethylene treatment assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes age-triggered and dark-induced leaf senescence, leading to faster chlorophyll decline and higher senescence progression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF3 transcript is induced at early stages of age-triggered and dark-induced senescence. 2. pif3 mutant retains higher chlorophyll, higher Fv/Fm, lower ion leakage, and lower H2O2 during dark-induced senescence. 3. pif3 shows delayed age-triggered senescence and reduced responsiveness to ethylene-induced senescence. 4. PIF3 overexpression accelerates senescence.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: up-regulated at early stage of leaf senescence and after 1–2 d dark treatment in rosette leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: early senescence-responsive in third/fourth rosette leaves; induced by darkness and aging
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-induced senescence; age-triggered senescence; exogenous ethylene treatment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Published microarray datasets cited; no accession reported in provided text for this study RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: delayed senescence may alter nutrient remobilization timing; specific yield tradeoff not reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to delay leaf senescence and maintain chlorophyll/photosynthetic competence, but possible effects on normal senescence-dependent nutrient remobilization should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct downstream targets were not experimentally established for PIF3 in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chlorophyll supports photosynthesis; ethylene promotes senescence signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; phytochrome-interacting factor (PIF) family
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of NYE1; transcriptional repression of GLK2; promotion of ethylene biosynthesis/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: chlorophyll degradation; chloroplast activity maintenance repression; dark-induced ethylene biosynthesis and senescence signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator of dark-induced and age-triggered senescence; strongest phenotype among tested pif mutants
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NYE1; GLK2; GLK1 (binding shown, transcriptional repression not significant in luciferase assay); ACS2; ACS6; ACS8; ACS9 (indirectly supported)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NYE1 promoter directly; represses GLK2 promoter directly; binds GLK1 promoter; positively affects dark induction of ACS2/ACS6/ACS8/ACS9 and ACC-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PIF4 may regulate GLK1 synergistically with BZR1 according to cited prior study; direct complex evidence not established here
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NYE1; GLK1; GLK2; ACS2; ACS6; ACS8; ACS9; BZR1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (pif4/SALK_140393C); overexpression analysis (35S::PIF4-Flash/MYC); RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; ChIP-PCR; Dual-Luciferase assay; ethylene quantification; chlorophyll measurement; Fv/Fm measurement; ion leakage assay; DAB staining; ethylene rescue assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes chlorophyll degradation and senescence; represses chloroplast maintenance; enhances dark-induced ethylene production and ethylene-triggered senescence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF4 is induced early during age-triggered and dark-induced senescence, and PIF4 protein accumulates in darkness. 2. pif4 shows the strongest delayed senescence phenotype, with higher chlorophyll, higher Fv/Fm, lower ion leakage, and lower H2O2. 3. RNA-seq indicates PIF4 promotes senescence-associated transcriptome changes, chlorophyll degradation genes, repression of photosynthesis genes, and ROS-responsive genes. 4. PIF4 directly activates NYE1 and directly represses GLK2 by promoter binding. 5. PIF4 promotes dark-induced ACS gene expression, ethylene accumulation, and ethylene-induced senescence.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced at early senescence stage; induced after 1–2 d dark treatment; protein stability increased after dark treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: early senescence-responsive in leaves; elevated around major switch time of senescence transcriptome (~29 DAS); induced in detached dark-treated rosette leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: darkness; age-triggered senescence; exogenous ethylene; detached leaf dark incubation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2500), accession NA in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: delaying PIF4 activity may preserve chlorophyll but could interfere with normal senescence and nutrient remobilization; explicit growth penalty not reported here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for engineering delayed leaf senescence/stay-green traits and altered ethylene-mediated senescence, but deployment should consider possible remobilization tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PIF4 bound both GLK1 and GLK2 promoters, but only GLK2 repression was significant in the dual-luciferase assay in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting; indicator of senescence status
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; phytochrome-interacting factor (PIF) family
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of senescence-associated chlorophyll loss and ethylene responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: chlorophyll degradation and senescence-associated metabolic remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive senescence mediator required for full age-triggered and dark-induced chlorophyll decline
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: supports dark induction of ACS genes and promotes senescence progression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: phytochromes (contextual family interaction implied by PIF identity)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (pif5-1/SALK_143659 and pif5-2/SALK_072306); overexpression analysis; chlorophyll measurement; Fv/Fm measurement; ion leakage assay; qRT-PCR; DAB staining; ethylene treatment assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes age-triggered and dark-induced leaf senescence, leading to faster chlorophyll decline
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF5 transcript is induced early during age-triggered and dark-induced senescence. 2. pif5 mutants retain more chlorophyll, higher Fv/Fm, and lower ion leakage during dark-induced senescence. 3. pif5-2 shows delayed age-triggered senescence. 4. Dark induction of ethylene biosynthetic genes is reduced to some extent in pif5 mutant, and ethylene-induced senescence is delayed. 5. PIF5 overexpression accelerates senescence.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: up-regulated at early stage of senescence and after 1–2 d dark treatment, then trends downward later
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: early senescence-responsive in rosette leaves under dark treatment and aging
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-induced senescence; age-triggered senescence; exogenous ethylene treatment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Published microarray datasets cited; no accession reported in provided text for this gene-specific analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: delayed senescence may alter nutrient remobilization timing; specific growth penalties not reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for extending leaf greenness and delaying senescence, with caution regarding possible remobilization consequences
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter targets of PIF5 were not identified in this paper.
